global_cues.us_markets: Wall Street closed lower May 4 on Middle East escalation: S&P 500 -0.41% to 7,200.75; Nasdaq -0.19% to 25,067.80; Dow -1.13% (-557.37 pts) to 48,941.90. Selloff triggered after UAE intercepted Iranian missiles, the first activation of UAE alert system since the US-Iran ceasefire began. Oil-sensitive sectors led declines.
global_cues.european_markets: Mixed and thin May 4: UK FTSE 100 closed for early spring bank holiday. Germany DAX +0.1%, France CAC 40 -0.1%, Italy FTSE MIB +0.4%, pan-European Stoxx 600 +0.1%. EU autos pressured after Trump tariff threat (25% on EU cars/trucks).
global_cues.asian_markets: Mostly positive May 5 morning but liquidity-thin: Japan (Nikkei) and mainland China closed for public holiday. Hong Kong Hang Seng +1.26% in afternoon trade. GIFT Nifty +155 pts (+0.65%) at ~24,254, signalling a positive open for Indian indices vs Nifty's 24,119 close.

fii_dii_summary: May 4 cash market: FIIs net BUYERS at +Rs 2,835.62 cr and DIIs net BUYERS at +Rs 4,764.16 cr — strong combined institutional bid (~Rs 7,600 cr) supporting the tape into May 5. Two-sided buying is the constructive read; watch if FIIs flip on any oil/INR shock.
earnings_calendar: LT (Larsen & Toubro) - May 5 (Q4 FY26 board meet + earnings call; revenue growth seen steady on order book, profit margins expected squeezed)
M&M (Mahindra & Mahindra) - May 5 (Q4 FY26 + FY26 audited results; auto bellwether, follows record April SUV vols 56,331 +8% YoY)
BHEL (Bharat Heavy Electricals) - May 4 (Q4 FY26 results + final dividend consideration — read-through into May 5 capex plays)
SWIGGY (Swiggy) - this week (Q4 FY26 + FY26 audited results, final dividend recommendation expected)
Note: 280+ Indian companies report Q4 FY26 in May 4-9 week; full result calendar dense across mid/large caps
macro_events: RBI repo rate held at 5.25% (Feb 2026 MPC); stance neutral — no scheduled MPC May 5
RBI FY26 CPI projection 2.1% (Q4 FY26 ~3.2%); FY27 Q1 CPI projected 4.0%, Q2 4.2% — disinflation tailwind intact
RBI FY26 real GDP growth seen 7.4%; FY27 Q1 6.9%, Q2 7.0% — RBI revised growth higher
New 5-year CPI target band reaffirmed at 4% +/- 2% (Apr 2026 to Mar 2031)
April auto sales (released May 1-4) — strong YoY momentum across PVs and 2-wheelers (see sector themes)
sector_themes: Auto strong on April sales beat: MARUTI +5% on record 1,87,704 domestic units (+35% YoY); BAJAJ-AUTO +3% (+40% YoY total sales); HEROMOTOCO total sales +85.4% YoY at 5,66,086 units; TATAMOTORS PV +31.1% YoY at 59,701 units; M&M total +14% YoY (SUV +8%); TVSMOTOR +7% YoY — sector tailwind ahead of M&M earnings today
Pharma outperforming: Nifty Pharma +1.02% to 23,505.95 — but watch margin pressure from elevated freight (+15-30%) and energy costs feeding into inventory cost
IT underperforming since Feb 2026 — global growth concerns and Iran-driven risk-off weighing; keep TCS, INFY, WIPRO, HCLTECH on watch for relative weakness

Banking mixed: PSU banks under pressure on liquidity/NIM concerns despite 2025 outperformance — CANBK, UNIONBANK, BANKBARODA, SBIN sensitive to FII flows; private banks (HDFCBANK, ICICIBANK, AXISBANK) holding better
Energy/Oil & Gas: elevated Brent ($107-110, briefly $114-126) is a double-edged sword — upstream ONGC, OIL benefit; OMC margins (IOC, BPCL, HPCL) at risk; gas plays IGL, MGL, GAIL exposed
Capex/Infra in focus ahead of LT earnings — read-through to LT, SIEMENS, ABB, BHEL, NTPC
risk_flags: Middle East escalation: UAE intercepted Iranian missiles May 4 — first since US-Iran ceasefire; risk of broader Gulf disruption is the dominant overnight risk
Crude oil spike: Brent ~$107-110 (briefly $114-126 intraday May 4); WTI ~$102 — sustained levels above $115 would pressure CAD, INR and OMC margins
USD/INR weakness: rupee at ~95.33 (May 4), -0.47% on the day, -2.55% over past month, -13.19% YoY — import-cost inflation risk; FII flow sensitivity high
Strait of Hormuz risk: Iran's Revolutionary Guard reportedly fired cruise missiles at US warships and commercial vessels — choke-point disruption is the tail risk for crude/freight
Global recession chatter: CNBC commentary flagging 'misplaced euphoria' on potential oil-shock-led recession
Fed on hold, data-dependent — no near-term US rate cut tailwind; dollar firmness keeps INR pressured
Liquidity-thin Asia session today (Japan + China closed) — low-volume tape can amplify single-name moves on news
brief_summary: Mixed setup: GIFT Nifty +0.65% suggests a positive open despite Wall Street's Iran-driven dip, with strong April auto sales (MARUTI record, BAJAJ-AUTO +40% YoY) and two-sided FII+DII buying (~Rs 7,600 cr May 4) underpinning sentiment. Today's tape is earnings-driven — LT and M&M Q4 FY26 results are the key events, with auto basket positioned strong heading into M&M. Primary risks remain elevated crude (~$110 Brent), INR weakness (~95.33) and any further Iran-Gulf escalation; thin Asia liquidity (Japan/China shut) argues for tighter risk on single-name conviction trades.
